title: Marlborough Gallery’s Former Chelsea Space Sells for $7.5 Million to Gazelli Art House
summary: Marlborough‘s former New York home, vacated after the venerable gallery’s 2024 closure, was recently sold for $7.5 million to Gazelli Art House. fullArticle:

				We use vendors that may also process your information to help provide our services.

Marlborough‘s former New York home, vacated after the venerable gallery’s 2024 closure, was recently sold for $7.5 million to Gazelli Art House. Founded in 1946 by Frank Lloyd and Harry Fischer, Marlborough operated for nearly 80 years and represented top-name contemporary artists like Lucian Freud and Francis Bacon before the outfit’s eventual demise.

Marlborough operated in its two-story space at 545 West 25th Street (also known as the legendary Chelsea Arts Tower), currently home to the FLAG Art Foundation and Glasshouse Chelsea. The gallery space clocks in at 9,228 square feet, and also includes a specialized ligh...
